reasoning: Peer set (helmets) uses muted desaturated metallic palette: grays, silvers, dark metals, occasional warm gold and cool teal — all low-to-mid saturation. Candidate is vivid cyan-blue at full saturation, a dramatic outlier. (1) OUTLINE WEIGHT: peers use clean 1px dark outlines; candidate outline visible but harder to read against bright blue fill — marginal. (2) OUTLINE COLOR: peers use near-black; candidate appears dark blue outline, slight deviation. (3) SHADOW DIRECTION: peers imply top-left light; candidate top-lit highlight plausible, consistent. (4) SHADOW COUNT: peers show 1-2 shadow layers; candidate appears to have 1 — acceptable. (5) HIGHLIGHT INTENSITY: FAIL — peers have subtle to moderate specular; candidate has very prominent white circular/grid highlight pattern dominating the face, intensity far exceeds peer norm. (6) COLOR TEMPERATURE: peers are cool-neutral metallic; candidate is intense cool blue, beyond the cool range peers establish — pushed to extreme. (7) DETAIL DENSITY: candidate shows a grid/mosaic internal texture not seen in peers; peers use clean smooth fills with minimal internal linework — inconsistent rendering approach. (8) PROPORTION: candidate sprite is rendered visibly larger than all peers in the comparison sheet even at ostensibly matching scale — notable size outlier. (9) RENDERING STYLE: peers are clean pixel art geometric armor shapes; candidate has a chibi-face quality (large white circular highlights read as 'eyes') giving a character-like rather than item-like appearance, inconsistent with peer register. (10) PALETTE OVERLAP: near zero — no grays, silvers, dark metals, or earthy tones shared with peers; the entire hue space is different. (11) CHARM LEVEL: peers have utilitarian pixel-art charm without explicit face/character features; candidate reads as a cute face-character which, while charming, is a different register from peer helmets. Overall: 5 of 11 dimensions fail or are marginal; color and scale issues are severe enough to reject.
